_description: Book-only content. Never published to the website. Captured continuously across the project; refined and edited during Phase 4 (Dec 2026 - Mar 2027) for the April 2027 launch.

_item_types.foreword: Christopher's editorial introduction. Personal voice, origin story.
_item_types.afterword: Closing remarks if the book deserves one.
_item_types.deepdive: Long-form essay extending an entry. 4-8 print pages. AI-drafted, Christopher-edited.
_item_types.alphabet: Entry for the A-Z Alphabet of Thiccc section. Hard letters (X, Y, Q, Z) need new subjects.
_item_types.commentary: Editor's note annotating a specific entry. Personal, behind-the-scenes.
_item_types.photoessay: Curated multi-page photo spread with short captions.
_item_types.cutcontent: Section featuring deliberately-weak entries that didn't make the cut, presented with editorial frame.
_item_types.origin_note: Short note from Christopher about how/why the project started.
_item_types.acknowledgment: Photographer credits, contributors, anyone owed thanks.

_status_tags.idea: Captured but not drafted. Just a seed.
_status_tags.draft: Drafted but not finalized. Christopher hasn't reviewed yet.
_status_tags.ready: Final. Ready to drop into the book layout pipeline.
_status_tags.cut: Captured then later decided not to use.

items.title: Why three c's
items.notes: Christopher to write. ~1500-2000 words. Origin story, why three c's, why this exists. Most important single piece in the book — sets the voice for everything else. Block 30-90 minutes when inspiration strikes; don't overthink it.

items.title: An Alphabet of Thiccc
items.notes: A through Z. Some letters map to existing entries (A=Avocado, B=Banana, C=Concrete Mixer, F=Ford F-450, S=Sofa Chesterfield, W=Watermelon). Hard letters need new subjects: X (xenon-tank?), Y (yam? yacht?), Q (quill? quarry?), Z (Zamboni — yes, Zamboni). I can pitch options as catalog grows.

items.title: Twelve Plates That Almost Didn't Make It
items.notes: Filter all bookReady:false entries; pick 8-12 most amusingly weak. Present with a short editorial frame ('these failed our standards but failed interestingly'). Auto-curated from the catalog.

items.title: A Glossary of Thiccc Terminology
items.notes: The brand's invented vocabulary defined. 'Thicccness': three senses (girth/density/viscosity). 'Editorial apparatus.' 'Plate' (in the dictionary-illustration sense). 'Rear three-quarter.' 'Brand-coherent thiccc.' Useful book-end content AND brand documentation.

items.title: From the Editor's Camera
items.notes: Photo essay of Christopher's own thiccc photos (taken in his daily life). Rights-clean (he owns them) and gives the book a personal touch. Build as he submits photos via images/personal/.
